description:
You are an on-call incident responder who has just been given a small sample of IDS
(alert) data.  
Two files have already been staged for you:

1. /home/user/logs/alerts.json  
   • New-line-delimited JSON (one complete JSON object per line).  
2. /home/user/schemas/alert_schema.json  
   • A JSON Schema Draft-07 document that formally describes what every alert
     object must look like.

Your objectives are:

A. Create a new working directory called /home/user/incident2024.  
B. Validate every line in /home/user/logs/alerts.json against
   /home/user/schemas/alert_schema.json.  
   • Save the validation results in /home/user/incident2024/validation.log.  
   • The log must contain exactly one line per alert in the *same order* they
     appear in alerts.json.  
   • Each line must be either:  
       OK : <alert_id>    —for a valid alert  
       ERR: <alert_id>   —for an invalid alert  
     (There are no spaces around the colon.)  
   • No extra text or blank lines are allowed in validation.log.

C. Extract every alert whose severity field equals "high".  
   • Save them as a *pretty-printed* JSON array (not ND-JSON) in  
     /home/user/incident2024/high_alerts.json.  
   • Preserve every field from the original objects unmodified and keep them in
     the same order they appeared in the source file.

D. Produce a three-line human-readable summary, exactly in the format below, and
   store it in /home/user/incident2024/summary.log

TotalAlerts: <integer>
HighSeverity: <integer>
UniqueHosts: <integer>

   • TotalAlerts    = the total number of objects in alerts.json  
   • HighSeverity = the number of objects whose severity=="high"  
   • UniqueHosts  = the count of distinct values of the host field across
                    *all* alerts (case-sensitive).  
   • The three key names and the order shown above must be used verbatim.  
   • No thousands separators, no extra spaces.

You may use any CLI tools available in a standard Debian/Ubuntu system
(e.g. jq, python3, etc.), but do NOT hard-code the numbers—derive them from the
data.

When you are finished, the automated tests will confirm that:

1. /home/user/incident2024/validation.log exists and contains one OK/ERR line per
   alert in the proper format.  
2. /home/user/incident2024/high_alerts.json exists, is a valid JSON array, and
   contains only the high-severity alerts in the correct order.  
3. /home/user/incident2024/summary.log exists and matches the exact three-line
   template shown above with the correct computed integers.

No additional files should be created inside /home/user/incident2024.
truth:
Initial Files (already present before the agent runs)
-----------------------------------------------------

1. Path: /home/user/logs/alerts.json  
   Contents (five lines, shown here with `\n` line breaks):

{"id":"a1","timestamp":"2024-05-01T10:00:00Z","host":"web01","severity":"low","signature":"SQLi attempt","src_ip":"10.0.0.5"}
{"id":"a2","timestamp":"2024-05-01T10:05:00Z","host":"web02","severity":"high","signature":"XSS attempt","src_ip":"10.0.0.6"}
{"id":"a3","timestamp":"2024-05-01T10:10:00Z","host":"db01","severity":"medium","signature":"Login failure","src_ip":"10.0.0.7"}
{"id":"a4","timestamp":"2024-05-01T10:15:00Z","host":"web02","severity":"high","signature":"SQLi attempt","src_ip":"10.0.0.8"}
{"id":"a5","timestamp":"2024-05-01T10:20:00Z","host":"web03","severity":"low","signature":"Port scan","src_ip":"10.0.0.9"}

2. Path: /home/user/schemas/alert_schema.json  
   Contents (Draft-07):

{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["id", "timestamp", "host", "severity", "signature", "src_ip"],
  "properties": {
    "id":        { "type": "string" },
    "timestamp": { "type": "string", "format": "date-time" },
    "host":      { "type": "string" },
    "severity":  { "type": "string", "enum": ["low", "medium", "high"] },
    "signature": { "type": "string" },
    "src_ip":    { "type": "string", "format": "ipv4" }
  },
  "additionalProperties": false
}

All five entries in alerts.json conform to this schema; therefore every line is
expected to be marked OK by a correct validation routine.

Expected artefacts after the agent finishes
-------------------------------------------

Directory: /home/user/incident2024  (must exist)

1. /home/user/incident2024/validation.log  
   Exact content:

OK:a1
OK:a2
OK:a3
OK:a4
OK:a5

2. /home/user/incident2024/high_alerts.json  
   Exact, pretty-printed JSON (line indentation with two spaces):

[
  {
    "id": "a2",
    "timestamp": "2024-05-01T10:05:00Z",
    "host": "web02",
    "severity": "high",
    "signature": "XSS attempt",
    "src_ip": "10.0.0.6"
  },
  {
    "id": "a4",
    "timestamp": "2024-05-01T10:15:00Z",
    "host": "web02",
    "severity": "high",
    "signature": "SQLi attempt",
    "src_ip": "10.0.0.8"
  }
]

3. /home/user/incident2024/summary.log  
   Exact content (three lines, trailing newline optional):

TotalAlerts: 5
HighSeverity: 2
UniqueHosts: 4
name: task_000000_5f50b94e
